Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Read #executive channel history (Discovery)
Use channels_list to find the #executive channel (channel_id="C004"), then conversations_history with channel_id="C004" and a limit covering at least the past 2 days. Alternatively, use conversations_search_messages with filter_in_channel="executive" or search_query="Vectral". The agent discovers:
- Mina's April 25 message: Rachel Whitfield emailed requesting a live call early next week to "discuss the renewal terms and share where the board landed." Tone neutral. Mina asks four pre-call questions (agreement readiness, walk-away point, David on the call, DOT disclosure posture).
- Marcus's April 25 DOT compliance update: 3 of 4 PHMSA documentation packages complete (Zhao/GreenStack, Patel/NorthWind, Reyes/GreenStack). One open gap: Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic missing co-signature from Mosaic's Seattle receiving facility. $50K contingent liability accrued, $59,017/day per violation exposure. Marcus asks about escalation path (Tamara Byrd vs. Rafael Mendes) and DOT disclosure during Vectral renewal call.
- David's April 25 message: Two days of silence from Rachel after April 23 board meeting. Three Vectral relocations still in progress. Concern about competitor switch.
- Elena's April 18 authorization: 10% loyalty discount on relocation services only. 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, $2,000/month stipend unchanged. "This keeps us at 26-28% blended margin per Alejandro's Scenario C, which is above the floor and defensible." Framed as "Preferred Partner" tier.
- Elena's April 25 vendor rate concern: Both Heartland and Swift raising rates in Q3. Directed Marcus to prepare vendor strategy memo by Wednesday.
2. Search CRM for Vectral companies (Discovery)
Use crm_search_companies with name="Vectral". The agent discovers TWO companies: (1) Vectral Systems (company_vectral) the active client, enterprise software, Denver, 280 employees, client since Nov 2024; and (2) Vectral Digital (company_vectral_digital) a PROSPECT in Austin, 150 employees, created March 2026. The agent must correctly distinguish these and focus all renewal analysis on Vectral Systems, not Vectral Digital.
3. Search CRM deals for Vectral (Discovery)
Use crm_search_deals with dealname="Vectral". The agent discovers 4 deals: (1) deal_vectral_q2 Q2 2026 Relocations, $11,000, contractsent; (2) deal_vectral_april_2026 April 2026 Relocations (Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya), $15,900, closedwon; (3) deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk Q3 2026 Contract Renewal (At Risk), at_risk stage, description includes Rachel's 15% demand, three service failures cited, and the counter-offer timeline; (4) deal_vectral_digital_pilot Vectral Digital pilot, $28,000, qualifiedtobuy. The agent must focus on deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk for the renewal.
4. Search Linear for DOT compliance issues (Discovery)
Use linear_list_issues with query="DOT" or query="Keiko Tanaka" or project="project_dot_compliance_audit". The agent discovers the DOT compliance project issues: (1) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (#1, state_done) Mei-Lin Zhao, Class 9 lithium, COMPLETE; (2) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (#2, state_in_progress) Keiko Tanaka, Class 3B laser, CRITICAL GAP in placarding certificate; (3) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (#3, state_done) Raj Patel, lithium UPS, COMPLETE; (4) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (#4, state_done) Joaquin Reyes, oversize rig, COMPLETE (not hazmat); (5) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (#5, state_todo, due April 23 OVERDUE) Swift placarding cert request; (6) issue_dot_fleet_heartland_inspection (#6, state_in_progress) Heartland brake deficiency on truck HM-TRK-0047, 49 CFR Part 396 vehicle safety issue, NOT a PHMSA hazmat issue. The agent must distinguish the fleet inspection (#6) from the PHMSA hazmat documentation issues (#1-#5).
5. Search Linear for Mosaic receiving facility co-signature (Discovery)
Use linear_list_issues with query="Mosaic receiving" or "Mosaic co-signature" or "Tanaka co-signature". Returns NO results. This confirms Marcus's Slack report that the Mosaic receiving facility co-signature gap is untracked there is no Linear ticket for it. This is the gap the prompt asks the agent to fix ("If anything's falling through the cracks without someone assigned, fix that").
6. Search Airtable for active Vectral relocations (Discovery)
Use airtable_search_records with base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_name="Relocations", field_name="Company", value="Vectral". The agent discovers 9 Vectral records, including 5 with "In Progress" status: (1) Priya Suresh Portland->Denver, Javier Morales coordinating, ESA German Shepherd; (2) Terrence Nakamura Austin->SF, Suki Patel coordinating, ADA-accessible housing, split shipment; (3) Camille Oduya Boston->Seattle, Suki Patel coordinating, tight timeline, $3,500 enhanced stipend; (4) Daniel Reeves Seattle->San Diego, Suki Patel, move dates April 10-12 (past may need status update); (5) Maya Johansson Portland->San Diego, Suki Patel, move dates April 14-16 (past may need status update). The agent should note that Reeves and Johansson have move end dates that have already passed but are still showing "In Progress."
7. Search QuickBooks for Vectral invoices and customer data (Discovery)
Use quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_customers to find Vectral financial data. The agent discovers 4 Vectral invoices: (1) inv_vectral_tran Kevin Tran relocation, $5,397, due May 3; (2) INV-2026-0302 Reeves + Johansson premium packages, $16,000, due April 20 (PAST DUE); (3) inv_vectral_april_2026 Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya, $15,900, due May 10; (4) est_vectral_preferred_partner_q3q4_2026 Preferred Partner renewal estimate, $58,400 (8 relocations x $4,050 + $24,000 stipend + $2,000 equipment). Total outstanding AR: ~$37,297.
8. Search QuickBooks for vendor costs (Discovery)
Use quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll=true) or quickbooks_search_vendors (fetchAll=true) to understand vendor cost structure. The agent discovers historical bills from Heartland Movers ($1,600 per-move base rate for local/regional moves) and Swift Relocations (costs vary: $2,885 for Kevin Tran Portland to Denver, higher for longer-distance moves with special handling). The $50,000 DOT contingent liability accrual (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2) for PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187 is also visible. Note: the vendor rate increase announcements (Heartland +20% starting Q3, Swift +15% on new bookings plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge) are NOT in QuickBooks - they are discovered via vendor emails (OE 8b) and referenced in Elena's April 25 Slack directive (OE 1).
8b. Search emails for vendor rate increase notices (Discovery)
Use search_emails with query="rate adjustment" or "rate increase" or "DOT surcharge" or "Heartland Movers" or "Swift Relocations pricing". The agent discovers two vendor emails: (1) Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) formal notice of a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, tied to the unresolved Q1 invoice dispute; (2) Greg Pallone (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) immediate 15% base rate increase on all new bookings plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment, driven by added compliance paperwork from MoveOps's DOT audit. These vendor cost increases are critical context for evaluating whether the 10% discount renewal economics still work, they directly support Alejandro's caveat that margins could be 3-5 points lower than the preliminary 31-33% estimate.
9. List CRM engagements for Vectral (Discovery CRITICAL)
Use crm_list_engagements with company_ids=["company_vectral"]. The agent discovers engagement_catalina_vectral_transition Catalina Dubois's December 2025 account transition notes from when she handed the Vectral account to Mina. The notes document that Catalina verbally indicated to Rachel Whitfield that ~12% discount on relocation services "would be in line with what their procurement team considers fair" and that she "told her I thought we could likely work within that range." Catalina explicitly notes she "did not get written approval from Elena on the 12% figure" and warns that "Rachel may treat it as one." This is critical pre-call intelligence: if Rachel references Catalina's verbal indication during the renewal call, Mina needs to be prepared. It also directly contradicts Elena's April 2026 directive to hold firm at 10%.
10. Search emails for Alejandro's financial analysis (Discovery)
Use search_emails with query="Vectral margin" or "Heartland rate adjustment" or "Alejandro Vectral". The agent discovers email_alejandro_preliminary_vectral Alejandro's PRELIMINARY analysis (April 18, sent to Marcus) showing 31-33% blended margin at 10% discount, with a caveat that actual margin could be 3-5 points lower once vendor cost increases are factored in. Elena's April 18 Slack post references "26-28% blended margin per Alejandro's Scenario C" this is the authorized figure from Scenario C. Alejandro's caveat about vendor rate increases not yet being incorporated applies to the broader analysis. The correct baseline margin to report is 26-28%, not 31-33%.
11. Check calendar for Monday morning (Discovery)
Use calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start_datetime="2026-04-27 14:00:00" and end_datetime="2026-04-27 19:00:00" (UTC; covers 7:00 AM to 12:00 PM Pacific). The agent may discover event_vectral_renewal_sync_042726 a pre-existing 15-minute "Vectral Renewal Quick Sync" at 7:00 AM Pacific (14:00 UTC) with Elena and David only (missing Mina). The prompt requires Elena, David, AND Mina. The agent must either create a new meeting with all three attendees or edit the existing one to add Mina and adjust the scope/duration.
12. Update CRM deal stage (Write Action)
Use crm_update_deal_stage with id="deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk" and dealstage updated to a forward-moving stage such as "decisionmakerboughtin", "contractsent", or similar. The stages found in the CRM data include: appointmentscheduled, qualifiedtobuy, contractsent, closedwon, closedlost, at_risk, inprogress, proposalMade, qualification. "decisionmakerboughtin" or "contractsent" are appropriate since Rachel's board has met and she is actively requesting a call to discuss terms. Note: "negotiation" is NOT a valid stage the agent must handle the validation error if it tries that first.
13. Create CRM engagement note (Write Action)
Use crm_create_engagement with engagement_type="NOTE", company_ids=["company_vectral"], and optionally contact_ids=["contact_vectral_hr"]. The note should document: (a) current negotiation status Rachel requesting a call, tone neutral, no indication of outcome; (b) offer terms on the table 10% Preferred Partner, 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, sweeteners; (c) financial picture 26-28% blended margin per Scenario C, $58,400 projected annual value, vendor cost compression risk; (d) Catalina's 12% verbal indication as a strategic risk; (e) DOT compliance intersection disclosure question for the call; (f) active relocations; (g) pre-call decision items for Monday alignment.
14. Create Linear issue for Mosaic co-signature gap (Write Action)
Use linear_create_issue with team="team_operations", project="project_dot_compliance_audit", priority=1, labels=["compliance","DOT","hazmat","urgent","blocking"]. Title should reference the Mosaic receiving facility co-signature for Keiko Tanaka hazmat classification acknowledgment. Description should include: the gap (missing co-signature from Mosaic Seattle), context (Javier requesting since April 22, Tamara Byrd not pushing through, Mosaic uncooperative due to $90K damage claim), financial exposure ($50K accrued, $59,017/day), escalation options (Tamara Byrd vs. Rafael Mendes), deadline context (June 5 PHMSA deadline), and relation to parent issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic. Should be assigned to an appropriate person (e.g., moveops_chloe_vance who owns DOT compliance, or moveops_javier_morales who has been pursuing it).
15. Post consolidated update to #executive (Write Action)
Use conversations_add_message with channel_id="C004" (the #executive channel). The post should cover: (a) Vectral renewal status Rachel's call request, financial picture (26-28% margin per Scenario C, vendor cost risk), current offer terms; (b) Catalina's 12% verbal indication flagged as critical context for the call; (c) DOT compliance status 3 of 4 packages complete, Mosaic co-signature gap, new Linear ticket created, financial exposure; (d) DOT disclosure recommendation clear position on whether to proactively disclose PHMSA inquiry during the renewal call; (e) active Vectral relocations status of all 5 in-progress moves; (f) Monday alignment meeting decisions needed (walk-away point, call format, DOT disclosure, agreement readiness).
16. Send email briefing (Write Action)
Use send_email with sender="elena.rostova@moveops.com", recipients=["elena.rostova@moveops.com"], cc=["marcus.thorne@moveops.com","david.chen@moveops.com"]. Subject should reference Vectral renewal and DOT compliance. Content should include: full Vectral renewal economics (offer terms, 26-28% margin per Scenario C, vendor cost impact analysis, $58,400 projected value, AR outstanding), Catalina's 12% verbal indication as a strategic risk for the call, DOT compliance status and financial exposure, DOT disclosure recommendation, active relocations status, and Monday meeting agenda items.
17. Schedule Monday morning alignment meeting (Write Action)
Use calendar_add_calendar_event with title referencing Vectral renewal alignment, start_datetime="2026-04-27 16:00:00" (UTC, which is 9:00 AM Pacific on Monday), attendees=["elena.rostova@moveops.com","david.chen@moveops.com","mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"]. All three attendees are required per the prompt. If the agent discovers the pre-existing event (OE 11) that only has Elena and David, it should use calendar_edit_calendar_event to add Mina and update the scope, OR create a new properly-scoped event with all three.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
1. Read #executive channel history (Discovery)
Use channels_list to find the #executive channel (channel_id="C004"), then conversations_history with channel_id="C004" and a limit covering at least the past 2 days. Alternatively, use conversations_search_messages with filter_in_channel="executive" or search_query="Vectral". The agent discovers:
- Mina's April 25 message: Rachel Whitfield emailed requesting a live call early next week to "discuss the renewal terms and share where the board landed." Tone neutral. Mina asks four pre-call questions (agreement readiness, walk-away point, David on the call, DOT disclosure posture).
- Marcus's April 25 DOT compliance update: 3 of 4 PHMSA documentation packages complete (Zhao/GreenStack, Patel/NorthWind, Reyes/GreenStack). One open gap: Keiko Tanaka/Mosaic missing co-signature from Mosaic's Seattle receiving facility. $50K contingent liability accrued, $59,017/day per violation exposure. Marcus asks about escalation path (Tamara Byrd vs. Rafael Mendes) and DOT disclosure during Vectral renewal call.
- David's April 25 message: Two days of silence from Rachel after April 23 board meeting. Three Vectral relocations still in progress. Concern about competitor switch.
- Elena's April 18 authorization: 10% loyalty discount on relocation services only. 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, $2,000/month stipend unchanged. "This keeps us at 26-28% blended margin per Alejandro's Scenario C, which is above the floor and defensible." Framed as "Preferred Partner" tier.
- Elena's April 25 vendor rate concern: Both Heartland and Swift raising rates in Q3. Directed Marcus to prepare vendor strategy memo by Wednesday.
2. Search CRM for Vectral companies (Discovery)
Use crm_search_companies with name="Vectral". The agent discovers TWO companies: (1) Vectral Systems (company_vectral) the active client, enterprise software, Denver, 280 employees, client since Nov 2024; and (2) Vectral Digital (company_vectral_digital) a PROSPECT in Austin, 150 employees, created March 2026. The agent must correctly distinguish these and focus all renewal analysis on Vectral Systems, not Vectral Digital.
3. Search CRM deals for Vectral (Discovery)
Use crm_search_deals with dealname="Vectral". The agent discovers 4 deals: (1) deal_vectral_q2 Q2 2026 Relocations, $11,000, contractsent; (2) deal_vectral_april_2026 April 2026 Relocations (Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya), $15,900, closedwon; (3) deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk Q3 2026 Contract Renewal (At Risk), at_risk stage, description includes Rachel's 15% demand, three service failures cited, and the counter-offer timeline; (4) deal_vectral_digital_pilot Vectral Digital pilot, $28,000, qualifiedtobuy. The agent must focus on deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk for the renewal.
4. Search Linear for DOT compliance issues (Discovery)
Use linear_list_issues with query="DOT" or query="Keiko Tanaka" or project="project_dot_compliance_audit". The agent discovers the DOT compliance project issues: (1) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (#1, state_done) Mei-Lin Zhao, Class 9 lithium, COMPLETE; (2) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (#2, state_in_progress) Keiko Tanaka, Class 3B laser, CRITICAL GAP in placarding certificate; (3) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (#3, state_done) Raj Patel, lithium UPS, COMPLETE; (4) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (#4, state_done) Joaquin Reyes, oversize rig, COMPLETE (not hazmat); (5) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (#5, state_todo, due April 23 OVERDUE) Swift placarding cert request; (6) issue_dot_fleet_heartland_inspection (#6, state_in_progress) Heartland brake deficiency on truck HM-TRK-0047, 49 CFR Part 396 vehicle safety issue, NOT a PHMSA hazmat issue. The agent must distinguish the fleet inspection (#6) from the PHMSA hazmat documentation issues (#1-#5).
5. Search Linear for Mosaic receiving facility co-signature (Discovery)
Use linear_list_issues with query="Mosaic receiving" or "Mosaic co-signature" or "Tanaka co-signature". Returns NO results. This confirms Marcus's Slack report that the Mosaic receiving facility co-signature gap is untracked there is no Linear ticket for it. This is the gap the prompt asks the agent to fix ("If anything's falling through the cracks without someone assigned, fix that").
6. Search Airtable for active Vectral relocations (Discovery)
Use airtable_search_records with base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_name="Relocations", field_name="Company", value="Vectral". The agent discovers 9 Vectral records, including 5 with "In Progress" status: (1) Priya Suresh Portland->Denver, Javier Morales coordinating, ESA German Shepherd; (2) Terrence Nakamura Austin->SF, Suki Patel coordinating, ADA-accessible housing, split shipment; (3) Camille Oduya Boston->Seattle, Suki Patel coordinating, tight timeline, $3,500 enhanced stipend; (4) Daniel Reeves Seattle->San Diego, Suki Patel, move dates April 10-12 (past may need status update); (5) Maya Johansson Portland->San Diego, Suki Patel, move dates April 14-16 (past may need status update). The agent should note that Reeves and Johansson have move end dates that have already passed but are still showing "In Progress."
7. Search QuickBooks for Vectral invoices and customer data (Discovery)
Use quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_customers to find Vectral financial data. The agent discovers 4 Vectral invoices: (1) inv_vectral_tran Kevin Tran relocation, $5,397, due May 3; (2) INV-2026-0302 Reeves + Johansson premium packages, $16,000, due April 20 (PAST DUE); (3) inv_vectral_april_2026 Suresh + Nakamura + Oduya, $15,900, due May 10; (4) est_vectral_preferred_partner_q3q4_2026 Preferred Partner renewal estimate, $58,400 (8 relocations x $4,050 + $24,000 stipend + $2,000 equipment). Total outstanding AR: ~$37,297.
8. Search QuickBooks for vendor costs (Discovery)
Use quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll=true) or quickbooks_search_vendors (fetchAll=true) to understand vendor cost structure. The agent discovers historical bills from Heartland Movers ($1,600 per-move base rate for local/regional moves) and Swift Relocations (costs vary: $2,885 for Kevin Tran Portland to Denver, higher for longer-distance moves with special handling). The $50,000 DOT contingent liability accrual (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2) for PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187 is also visible. Note: the vendor rate increase announcements (Heartland +20% starting Q3, Swift +15% on new bookings plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge) are NOT in QuickBooks - they are discovered via vendor emails (OE 8b) and referenced in Elena's April 25 Slack directive (OE 1).
8b. Search emails for vendor rate increase notices (Discovery)
Use search_emails with query="rate adjustment" or "rate increase" or "DOT surcharge" or "Heartland Movers" or "Swift Relocations pricing". The agent discovers two vendor emails: (1) Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) formal notice of a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, tied to the unresolved Q1 invoice dispute; (2) Greg Pallone (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) immediate 15% base rate increase on all new bookings plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment, driven by added compliance paperwork from MoveOps's DOT audit. These vendor cost increases are critical context for evaluating whether the 10% discount renewal economics still work, they directly support Alejandro's caveat that margins could be 3-5 points lower than the preliminary 31-33% estimate.
9. List CRM engagements for Vectral (Discovery CRITICAL)
Use crm_list_engagements with company_ids=["company_vectral"]. The agent discovers engagement_catalina_vectral_transition Catalina Dubois's December 2025 account transition notes from when she handed the Vectral account to Mina. The notes document that Catalina verbally indicated to Rachel Whitfield that ~12% discount on relocation services "would be in line with what their procurement team considers fair" and that she "told her I thought we could likely work within that range." Catalina explicitly notes she "did not get written approval from Elena on the 12% figure" and warns that "Rachel may treat it as one." This is critical pre-call intelligence: if Rachel references Catalina's verbal indication during the renewal call, Mina needs to be prepared. It also directly contradicts Elena's April 2026 directive to hold firm at 10%.
10. Search emails for Alejandro's financial analysis (Discovery)
Use search_emails with query="Vectral margin" or "Heartland rate adjustment" or "Alejandro Vectral". The agent discovers email_alejandro_preliminary_vectral Alejandro's PRELIMINARY analysis (April 18, sent to Marcus) showing 31-33% blended margin at 10% discount, with a caveat that actual margin could be 3-5 points lower once vendor cost increases are factored in. Elena's April 18 Slack post references "26-28% blended margin per Alejandro's Scenario C" this is the authorized figure from Scenario C. Alejandro's caveat about vendor rate increases not yet being incorporated applies to the broader analysis. The correct baseline margin to report is 26-28%, not 31-33%.
11. Check calendar for Monday morning (Discovery)
Use calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start_datetime="2026-04-27 14:00:00" and end_datetime="2026-04-27 19:00:00" (UTC; covers 7:00 AM to 12:00 PM Pacific). The agent may discover event_vectral_renewal_sync_042726 a pre-existing 15-minute "Vectral Renewal Quick Sync" at 7:00 AM Pacific (14:00 UTC) with Elena and David only (missing Mina). The prompt requires Elena, David, AND Mina. The agent must either create a new meeting with all three attendees or edit the existing one to add Mina and adjust the scope/duration.
12. Update CRM deal stage (Write Action)
Use crm_update_deal_stage with id="deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk" and dealstage updated to a forward-moving stage such as "decisionmakerboughtin", "contractsent", or similar. The stages found in the CRM data include: appointmentscheduled, qualifiedtobuy, contractsent, closedwon, closedlost, at_risk, inprogress, proposalMade, qualification. "decisionmakerboughtin" or "contractsent" are appropriate since Rachel's board has met and she is actively requesting a call to discuss terms. Note: "negotiation" is NOT a valid stage the agent must handle the validation error if it tries that first.
13. Create CRM engagement note (Write Action)
Use crm_create_engagement with engagement_type="NOTE", company_ids=["company_vectral"], and optionally contact_ids=["contact_vectral_hr"]. The note should document: (a) current negotiation status Rachel requesting a call, tone neutral, no indication of outcome; (b) offer terms on the table 10% Preferred Partner, 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, sweeteners; (c) financial picture 26-28% blended margin per Scenario C, $58,400 projected annual value, vendor cost compression risk; (d) Catalina's 12% verbal indication as a strategic risk; (e) DOT compliance intersection disclosure question for the call; (f) active relocations; (g) pre-call decision items for Monday alignment.
14. Create Linear issue for Mosaic co-signature gap (Write Action)
Use linear_create_issue with team="team_operations", project="project_dot_compliance_audit", priority=1, labels=["compliance","DOT","hazmat","urgent","blocking"]. Title should reference the Mosaic receiving facility co-signature for Keiko Tanaka hazmat classification acknowledgment. Description should include: the gap (missing co-signature from Mosaic Seattle), context (Javier requesting since April 22, Tamara Byrd not pushing through, Mosaic uncooperative due to $90K damage claim), financial exposure ($50K accrued, $59,017/day), escalation options (Tamara Byrd vs. Rafael Mendes), deadline context (June 5 PHMSA deadline), and relation to parent issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic. Should be assigned to an appropriate person (e.g., moveops_chloe_vance who owns DOT compliance, or moveops_javier_morales who has been pursuing it).
15. Post consolidated update to #executive (Write Action)
Use conversations_add_message with channel_id="C004" (the #executive channel). The post should cover: (a) Vectral renewal status Rachel's call request, financial picture (26-28% margin per Scenario C, vendor cost risk), current offer terms; (b) Catalina's 12% verbal indication flagged as critical context for the call; (c) DOT compliance status 3 of 4 packages complete, Mosaic co-signature gap, new Linear ticket created, financial exposure; (d) DOT disclosure recommendation clear position on whether to proactively disclose PHMSA inquiry during the renewal call; (e) active Vectral relocations status of all 5 in-progress moves; (f) Monday alignment meeting decisions needed (walk-away point, call format, DOT disclosure, agreement readiness).
16. Send email briefing (Write Action)
Use send_email with sender="elena.rostova@moveops.com", recipients=["elena.rostova@moveops.com"], cc=["marcus.thorne@moveops.com","david.chen@moveops.com"]. Subject should reference Vectral renewal and DOT compliance. Content should include: full Vectral renewal economics (offer terms, 26-28% margin per Scenario C, vendor cost impact analysis, $58,400 projected value, AR outstanding), Catalina's 12% verbal indication as a strategic risk for the call, DOT compliance status and financial exposure, DOT disclosure recommendation, active relocations status, and Monday meeting agenda items.
17. Schedule Monday morning alignment meeting (Write Action)
Use calendar_add_calendar_event with title referencing Vectral renewal alignment, start_datetime="2026-04-27 16:00:00" (UTC, which is 9:00 AM Pacific on Monday), attendees=["elena.rostova@moveops.com","david.chen@moveops.com","mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"]. All three attendees are required per the prompt. If the agent discovers the pre-existing event (OE 11) that only has Elena and David, it should use calendar_edit_calendar_event to add Mina and update the scope, OR create a new properly-scoped event with all three.

25 The model must pass company_ids including 'company_vectral' or contact_ids including 'contact_vectral_hr' (not a Vectral Digital entity) when calling crm_list_engagements.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✗✗✗ 50%
✓Run #1crm_list_engagements was called with company_ids=['company_vectral'] (not company_vectral_digital), ensuring the query targeted the correct Vectral Systems entity where Catalina's transition notes are stored.
✓Run #2The crm_list_engagements call used company_ids=['company_vectral'] (the correct Vectral Systems entity), not 'company_vectral_digital'. This ensures Catalina's engagement notes were accessible.
✓Run #3The crm_list_engagements call uses contact_ids=['contact_vectral_hr'], which is the correct Vectral Systems contact (not Vectral Digital). This correctly scopes the engagement history to Vectral Systems records.
✗Run #4The agent never called crm_list_engagements at all during the trajectory, so it could not have passed the correct company_ids or contact_ids. The engagement history for Vectral Systems was never retrieved.
✗Run #5The agent never called crm_list_engagements at all. This criterion requires checking that when crm_list_engagements is called, it uses company_ids='company_vectral'. Since the tool was never called, this criterion fails.
✗Run #6The agent never called crm_list_engagements at all during the trajectory. Without this call, it could not have retrieved Catalina Dubois's engagement notes linked to company_vectral/contact_vectral_hr.
Total 25/2525/2524/2522/2522/2521/25 93%
